reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — desktop+mobile screenshots + conformance runner evidence

rubric.evidence: conformance (webgpu, headless no-GPU): loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. Source runs SmolVLM through transformers.js in a Web Worker (models/smolvlm-vision-language/worker.js + smolvlm.js), streaming tokens.
rubric.notes: Genuinely a WebGPU VLM (SigLIP encoder + SmolLM2 decoder). On a real GPU it streams a live answer; on this no-GPU runner it correctly shows the unsupported state instead of faking one.

rubric.evidence: The 'See inside' panel exposes the true I/O shape: the constructed chat template (with the <image> placeholder expanded to patch tokens) and a live token stream with per-token arrival times. io-shape-semantic=manual (needs a GPU device to see real output).
rubric.notes: Expected VLM output shape is explicit and legible. Semantic sanity of a real answer awaits a WebGPU device.

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id=pass (HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct linked), declares-task=pass (image-text-to-text), declares-backend=pass, declares-quantisation=pass (q4f16), inference-off-main-thread=pass. Desktop screenshot confirms at-a-glance: 'WebGPU (required — needs a GPU + shader-f16)'.
rubric.notes: Backend requirement (WebGPU + shader-f16) is stated up front — honest about the device bar.

rubric.evidence: shared-auto-init-loader=pass. Desktop screenshot shows the loader detail line 'HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct · transformers.js · ~250 MB · 0 MB cached of 10737 MB' — accurate cache accounting, no auto-download.
rubric.notes: auto-init-on-cached (warm-cache returning user) still to be exercised on a GPU device.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass, webgpu-capability-honest=blocked. Desktop screenshot shows the exact honest fallback: "This model can't run on this device/browser. It needs WebGPU (no GPU adapter here). Enable it via chrome://gpu / a WebGPU-capable browser."
rubric.notes: Textbook honest capability handling — a labelled needs-WebGPU explanation with enable steps, no blank panel, no faked output. blocked (not fail) is the correct, honest outcome on a no-GPU runner.

rubric.evidence: Real controls: sample-image strip, drop/upload zone (role=button, aria-label), prompt text input, five quick-prompt chips, Generate button, max-tokens number input, streaming answer region (aria-live). controls-operable=manual.
rubric.notes: Controls are correctly disabled until the model is ready (honest on a no-GPU device). A full keyboard/focus pass belongs on a WebGPU device where controls enable.

rubric.evidence: Desktop screenshot: the answer area shows placeholder 'The model's answer will stream here.' and the loader shows the needs-WebGPU state — nothing is fabricated as model output.
conformance: images-have-alt=pass (sample images have descriptive alt), buttons-have-names=pass. Dropzone is a labelled role=button; answer + status use aria-live.
rubric.notes: Strong. Screen-reader wording of the streaming token panel could be reviewed on a GPU device.

rubric.evidence: conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass. Desktop screenshot shows the two-column image/answer grid; mobile screenshot (matrix run) shows a single-column reflow with no horizontal overflow.
rubric.notes: Layout parity good on both classes. Because the interactive path is WebGPU-gated, a full mobile interaction pass requires a mobile GPU device; the unsupported-state layout itself is clean on both.

rubric.evidence: Generation runs in a Web Worker with token streaming; the 'See inside' panel surfaces per-token timing (a built-in latency readout). inp-stays-low=manual.
rubric.notes: Streaming + off-thread generation is INP-friendly by construction; measure tok/s + INP on a real GPU.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy work off the main thread / stream so generation doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — SmolVLM generation runs in a Web Worker and streams tokens incrementally to the answer region.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; token-stream panel in the page

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep long generation loops off the main thread and surface progress so interaction stays responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — worker-based streaming; controls disabled until ready to avoid mid-load interaction jank.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js streaming; disabled Generate button until onReady

guidanceConsulted.query: honest capability fallback for a WebGPU-only feature
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Detect the capability with a real adapter probe and show a labelled unsupported state with enable steps — never a blank panel.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — createModelLoader probes navigator.gpu.requestAdapter(); no adapter => labelled needs-WebGPU message with chrome://gpu steps.

openQuestions: On a WebGPU device: does SmolVLM produce a coherent answer that actually references the given image + prompt, streaming token by token?
Is the demo usable on a mobile WebGPU device (memory + tok/s), or should mobile be recorded unsupported with a memory-requirement evidence note?
Do the disabled controls expose an accessible explanation of WHY they're disabled (tied to the needs-WebGPU status)?

followUpGoals.goal: GPU-device matrix pass for smolvlm-vision-language: confirm streaming output + controls on desktop WebGPU; assess mobile WebGPU memory feasibility, then set support (ok, or mobile:unsupported+memory-evidence).
Add a multi-model 'screenshot Q&A + speak the answer' demo composing SmolVLM with a browser TTS model (e.g. Kokoro).

summary: smolvlm-vision-language is a genuine in-browser VLM with exemplary honest capability handling: on a no-GPU runner it shows a clear needs-WebGPU explanation with enable steps and accurate cache accounting (0 MB cached of 10737 MB), never a faked answer — the conformance webgpu assertion is correctly blocked, not failed. Runtime disclosure, accessibility, and layout parity are strong. Because the interactive path is WebGPU-gated, the semantic-output + full interaction checks (and a mobile-WebGPU memory-feasibility call) need a GPU device before support flips from needs-review. Revision 2, hand-reviewed from screenshots + runner evidence.
